zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Spencer於1462年出生在英國瓦維其可爾霍德爾時，她的父親Sir John Spencer of Hodnell27歲，母親Anne Empson24歲。她和William Saunders Esq至少有4個女兒。她去世於1526年2月12日在英國奧可福爾班波瑞享年64歲，埋葬在英國奧可福爾班波瑞。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane Spencer于1462年出生在英国瓦維其可爾霍德爾时，她的父亲Sir John Spencer of Hodnell27岁，母亲Anne Empson24岁。她和William Saunders Esq至少有4个女儿。她去世于1526年2月12日在英国奧可福爾班波瑞享年64岁，埋葬在英国奧可福爾班波瑞。

nameMeanings.name: Spencer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): occupational name for someone who dispensed provisions or money, from Middle English spenser(e), spencer(e) ‘household steward, butler, almoner’ (Anglo-Norman French espenser, Old French despensier). Compare Spence and Spender . There is some dispute about the origins of the Spencer family, whose most famous member in recent times was the late Princess of Wales, born Lady Diana Spencer (1961–97). Some sources say that they are descended from William the Conqueror's steward, Robert Despencer. What is clear is that by the 15th century they had become prosperous from sheep farming in Northamptonshire. Robert Spencer (died 1627) was said to be the wealthiest man in England. Their titles have included Earls of Sunderland and Earls Spencer; and through the female line the 5th Earl of Sunderland also became Duke of Marlborough in 1733. This connection was the result of the marriage, in 1700, of the 3rd Duke of Sunderland to the daughter of John Churchill, 1st Duke of Marlborough. The youngest son of this union, John Spencer (1708–46), was the father of the 1st Earl Spencer (1734–83).

variants.name: Spence
meanings: English and Scottish: nickname for a servant employed in the pantry of a great house or monastery, from Middle English spense, spence ‘pantry, larder, storeroom’ (Old French despense, from a Late Latin derivative of dispendere, past participle dispensus ‘to weigh out or dispense’). Compare Spencer .

variants.name: Spender
meanings: occupational name or status name from Middle English spender(e), spendour ‘steward, treasurer’, a shortened form of Middle English, Old French despendour, denoting someone who managed money or supplies. Compare Spencer .
alternatively, perhaps a nickname from Middle English spender(e), spendour ‘spender (of money), spendthrift’.

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Spencer was born in 1462, in Hodnell, Warwickshire, England, her father, Sir John Spencer of Hodnell, was 27 and her mother, Anne Empson, was 24. She had at least 4 daughters with William Saunders Esq. She died on 12 February 1526, in Banbury, Oxfordshire, England, at the age of 64, and was buried in Banbury, Oxfordshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Spencer est née en 1462, à Hodnell, Warwickshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir John Spencer of Hodnell, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Anne Empson, avait 24 ans. Elle a eu au moins 4 filles avec William Saunders Esq. Elle est décédée le 12 février 1526, à Banbury, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 64 ans, et a été enterrée à Banbury, Oxfordshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jane Spencer 1462 in Hodnell, Warwickshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir John Spencer of Hodnell 27 und ihre Mutter Anne Empson 24. Sie hatte mit William Saunders Esq mindestens 4 Töchter. Sie starb am 12. Februar 1526 im Alter von 64 Jahren in Banbury, Oxfordshire, England und wurde in Banbury, Oxfordshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Spencer è nata il nel 1462 nel Hodnell, Warwickshire, England, sua padre, Sir John Spencer of Hodnell, aveva 27 anni e sua madre, Anne Empson, aveva 24 anni. Lei avuto almeno 4 figlie con William Saunders Esq. Morì il 12 febbraio 1526, nel Banbury, Oxfordshire, England, all'età di 64 anni, e fu sepolta a Banbury, Oxfordshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Spencerが1462年にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアホードネルで生まれた時，父Sir John Spencer of Hodnellは27歳，母Anne Empsonは24歳であった。彼女はWilliam Saunders Esqとの間に少なくとも4人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1526年2月12日にイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアバンブリーで64歳で死去し，イギリスオクスフォードシュアイアバンブリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Spencer(이)가 1462년 영국 워윅실르 호드넬에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John Spencer of Hodnell(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Anne Empson(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 William Saunders Esq(와)과 적어도 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1526년 2월 12일 영국 스퍼드실르 반버이에서 64세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스퍼드실르 반버이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Spencer nasceu em 1462, em Hodnell, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John Spencer of Hodnell, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Anne Empson, tinha 24 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 4 filhas com William Saunders Esq. Ela faleceu em 12 de fevereiro de 1526, em Banbury, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, com 64 anos, e foi sepultada em Banbury, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Spencer родилась в 1462 г. в местности ходнелл, варвиккшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir John Spencer of Hodnell было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Anne Empson, 24. У неё было по крайней мере 4 дочери от William Saunders Esq. Она умерла 12 февраля 1526 г. в местности банбури, оксфордшир, Англия в возрасте 64 лет и была похоронена в местности банбури, оксфордшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jane Spencer nació en 1462, en Hodnell, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir John Spencer of Hodnell, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Anne Empson, tenía 24 años. Tuvo por lo menos 4 hijas con William Saunders Esq. Murió el 12 de febrero de 1526, en Banbury, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 64 años, y fue sepultada en Banbury, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra.
